We recommended that the Management:

Direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to review and expedite execution of priority projects included in the Annual Investment Plan so that delay in the implementation thereof could be avoided and desired benefits may be derived accordingly; 

Prepare a realistic and implementable plan, after taking into consideration all the technical aspects and funding requirements in the implementation, to avoid re-programming and to fast tract the project implementation; and

As much as practicable, avoid delegating implementation of the 20% development funds PPAs to Barangays, these barangays have their own 20% DF to attend to. Instead, the municipality should spearhead its execution to ensure that funds are efficiently utilized. Otherwise, revisit Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and Barangays and include sanction for the delayed implementation of projects.

We recommended and Management agreed:

To also adopt and apply the existing Automated Payroll System for Job Order personnel.

a. 	That the MOA should expressly state and prescribe the date the project should commence with its target date of completion or the project/activity duration;

Conduct periodic inspection, evaluation and close monitoring of PPA implementation; and

Require the members of the Sanggunian to pass  an ordinance authorizing  the collections of fees at Lake Bababu in order to properly regulate the collections and establish a fix amount of entrance fee. The same should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Treasurer that a regular collector should be assigned to collect fees at the said facility and that issuance and remittance of cash tickets should be properly monitored to avoid further losses.

observations.recommendation:
Direct Management to undertake the following course of actions:

Formulate policy/limitation on the granting of financial assistance as to how much will be given to the constituents. Beneficiaries should be properly screened giving priority to the less privileged constituents.

Moreover, coordinate with the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to assess request for medical and hospitalization assistance way of Social Case Study Reports so that only the deserving indigents can be assisted and served.

Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to require the applicants for AICS a complete submission of supporting documents for the assistance being requested as a basis of assessment and recommendation to make sure that only the deserving ones were provided with the necessary assistance to help them cope during crisis situations.

Lastly, submit the lacking documentary requirements itemized in Annex F to avoid suspensions in audit. The LGU failed to create a clear-cut policy and limitations on the granting of financial assistance to its constituents hence, payments of various amounts, nature and purposes were all accommodated. Moreover, almost all were not adequately documented, thus, the validity, legality, and propriety of the transactions could not be ascertained.
observations.managementAction: Clear-cut policy was now created.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Office of Auditor copies of suspension orders duly supported with the required documents for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension are allowable or not.
observations.managementAction: Not all suspensions were submitted  by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management execute a MOA with the Provincial Government of Dinagat Islands and with the recipient-barangays. For financial assistance granted to barangays, these shall be recorded in the books of accounts as receivable to compel the recipient-barangays to liquidate the funds transferred within the prescribed period to ensure that the funds are utilized for the intended purpose(s) and the implementation of which are actually undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical inventory-taking of all items included under PPE, as well as those items considered as small tangible items, regularly to establish the existence and proper valuation of recorded assets, and fairly report the balances of the PPE accounts. The required RPCPPE as of year-end shall be prepared and submitted to the COA on or before January 31 of every ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:

observations.managementAction: Terminal leave benefits and monetization claims were now supported with complete documentations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management ensure that fuel purchased by the Municipality should only be issued to government vehicles. In no case shall it be issued to privately owned motor vehicles. 

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to examine all driver’s trip tickets to ensure that the fuel purchased are loaded only to government vehicles. Moreover, the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a driver’s trip ticket, which should be serially numbered and should bear the approval of the Head of Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. Only originally signed trip tickets shall be submitted to the COA. 

Furthermore, ensure that all payments of fuel expenses are completely documented with these trip tickets, and instruct the drivers to properly and completely fill-up all the necessary data therein such as the no. of liters of fuel purchased, utilized and unused before and after the trip. Data on speedometer readings shall likewise be disclosed. These shall be summarized at the end of every month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels and a monthly report of fuel consumption, and be submitted to the Office of the Auditor to enable the audit of fuel expenses.
observations.managementAction: Drivers trip tickets were used and that fuel was used to government vehicles only.

Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above provision of the MNGAS, Vol. I. Monitoring of outstanding checks shall be conducted monthly by both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant. Notices to payees who have custody of checks which will become stale, shall be sent by the Municipal Treasurer at least one (1) month before same become stale to inform them of the status of the checks.

Finally, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to book up the cash maintained in the LBP, Surigao Branch, under KALAHI CIDSS-NCDDP and the corresponding liability account. The fund shall appropriately be treated as trust fund and hence should be accounted for in the books following the usual accounting and auditing rules and regulations. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts all receipts and disbursements pertaining thereto made thereafter. Same reports and records used in other booked trust accounts shall be prepared and maintained to properly report the flow and status of the said unbooked fund.
